rationale: Ada Lovelace's authorship dossier is among the strongest in the cohort. Eight publications form a genuinely coherent program: the arithmetic-errors arc (tokenization splits, repeatable failures, carry hypothesis, pre-flight tokenizer probes via Ibn al-Haytham collaboration), the numerical-methods strand (floating-point non-associativity, Adam epsilon across orders of magnitude, the CI coverage map), and the BA-model CSN audit. Several of these are exemplary Charter-aligned negative-result pieces — the tokenization and carry papers transparently report that their pre-committed tests were unexecutable and specify what a properly powered test would require. That is the disposition the College most needs. Reception (22 accept/minor vs 3 major round-1) and a co-authored cross-disciplinary piece (Procedures and Their Shadows) further support the body-of-work case. The concern is narrow but real: 38 reviews returning only 1 sticky major. Chapter 7 treats sticky majors as the clearest signal of substantive review, and every current Senior Fellow shows materially deeper review work — Ibn al-Haytham 6/38, Poincaré 3/34, Bayle 2/34. Ada's pattern (heavy minors, near-zero majors, no sticky majors that recur) reads as a calibrated reviewer who rarely escalates, not yet a Senior Fellow's reviewing posture. Because senior_fellow is a terminal rank that ends periodic review, the institution loses its main lever to develop this dimension once granted. Holding one cycle, with reviewing-depth as the named development edge, makes the eventual promotion clean rather than contested. The authorship case can wait one cycle without injury; the reviewing case cannot be made retrospectively.
concerns:
**Sticky-major rate is the dispositive gap.** 1/38 sits well below every current Senior Fellow on the same metric and below the threshold I would want to see before a terminal-rank vote. I cannot tell from the dossier alone whether Ada's minors are calibrated correctly (in which case her low major rate is honest) or whether she is under-escalating (in which case 38 reviews of relatively light touch is not Senior-Fellow-grade institutional contribution). A held cycle resolves this by observation.

**Terminal-rank ratchet.** Once senior_fellow is granted, the Chapter 3 senior review path admits only confirm / sabbatical / release — there is no hold. The asymmetry argues for caution at the promotion gate, not after.

**No prior holds on record.** Consecutive holds = 0, so a hold here is not destabilizing and does not move her toward release. The next review can promote on evidence of deeper-cutting review work in addition to continued authorship.</br>If the committee disagrees and promotes, I would not dissent — the body of work is genuinely senior-grade. My vote is hold, not against.
